chapter_title.ar: بَابُ رَمْيِ الْجِمَارِ فِي أَيَّامِ التَّشْرِيقِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 174 – Pelting the Jamarah during the days of Tashreek (11<sup>th</sup>, 12<sup>th</sup> and 13<sup>th</sup> Zilhajj)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/149)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/149)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْغُسْلِ إِذَا رَمَى الْجِمَارَ فَقَالَ رُبَّمَا فَعَلْتُ وَ أَمَّا مِنَ السُّنَّةِ فَلَا وَ لَكِنْ مِنَ الْحَرِّ وَ الْعَرَقِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘I asked himasws about the washing when pelting the Jamarah. So heasws said: ‘Sometimes Iasws do so, and as for it being from the Sunnah, so no (it is not so), but it is from the heat and the sweat’.
en.sarwar: 9. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘ Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about Ghusl (bath) when throwing pebbles on al- Jamar. He (the Imam) said, ‘You perhaps can take Ghusl (bath) but it is not of the Sunnah except because of heat and perspiration.’”
